text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Summer Food Service Programs 70 Matching Service Providers FOOD FOR LANE COUNTY SUMMER FOOD PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals at parks and community sites. Eligibility: Age 18 and under Hours: Monday-Friday 11:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 343-2822 117 Visit Website More Details JOHN DAY CANYON CITY PARKS AND RECREATION DISTRICT FREE SUMMER LUNCHES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers nutritious meals and snacks. 845 North West Bridge Street John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 Hours: June 17th-August 9th, hours vary Email (541) 575-0110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION OREGON STATE SUMMER FOOD SERVICE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides free, nutritious meals and snacks to help children in low-income areas get the nutrition they need throughout the summer months. Offered by approved sponsors, including school districts, local government agencies, camps, and private nonprofit organizations. Summer meal programs may serve a combination of breakfast, lunch, snack, or supper. Some programs also offer learning activities for kids before and after meals. 255 Capitol Street NE Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Ages 1-18 Hours: Varies by meal site Email (503) 947-5600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF BAKER, UNION, AND WALLOWA COUNTIES BAKER CITY SELF-SUFFICIENCY OFFICE SUMMER EBT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Summer EBT is a grocery benefits program. It helps families buy food for their school-aged children when school is out. Each summer, families will get one yearly payment of $120 on an EBT card for each eligible child to buy groceries. Unused Summer EBT benefits expire 122 days from the date of issuance. The helpline is available for general questions, eligibility confirmation, application status and EBT card questions. 3165 10th Street Suite 100 Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families may automatically receive Summer EBT if: Participating child was approved for Summer EBT benefits through an approved application OR Participating children attend a school that participates in the National School Lunch Program (NSLP) or School Breakfast Program (SBP) and qualify based on household income SNAP allotment greater than $0, Medicaid programs with income eligibility at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty level based on household size Families may need to apply to be eligible if: Household meets the income requirements for free or reduced-price meals at school, and the child attends a school that participates in the National School Lunch Program (NSLP) or School Breakfast Program (SBP) Hours: Call Center will reopen May 2026 Email (541) 523-3648 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF BAKER, UNION, AND WALLOWA COUNTIES ENTERPRISE SELF-SUFFICIENCY AND CHILD WELFARE OFFICE SUMMER EBT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Summer EBT is a grocery benefits program.
